- Polyether / aza-crown scaffolds → MeOH or MeCN
- Hydrogen-bond donors/acceptors without water solubility → DMSO

This is almost certainly a para-sulfonatocalix[4]arene or a derivative. para-sulfonatocalix[4]arene is a well-known water-soluble host. It has a hydrophobic cavity (aromatic walls) and four sulfonate groups at the upper rim (portal) which are negatively charged at neutral pH, making it water-soluble. General principles given:
- Large hydrophobic cavities with polar portals (e.g. glycoluril-based, sulfonated) → water
- Neutral macrocycles with aromatic walls, no charged groups → CHCl3 or CH2Cl2
- Polyether / aza-crown scaffolds → MeOH or MeCN
- Hydrogen-bond donors/acceptors without water solubility → DMSO
